title: rubus × consanguineus
intro: Rubus × consanguineus, also known as the American dewberry or Pacific dewberry, is a hybrid of the blackberry (Rubus argutus) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its anthocyanin content, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × consanguineus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus × consanguineus may be made into teas, tinctures, or dried extracts, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
